data.body:
INTERMEDIATE investigation complete (confidence: medium→revised after empirical testing).

EMPIRICAL TEST RESULTS (2026-04-16, parallel dispatch, same session):

Control (isolation omitted):
- sub-agent PWD: orchestrator root (same as caller)
- sub-agent git rev-parse: orchestrator root
- canary-NOISO.txt created: landed in orchestrator session tree

Isolated (isolation='worktree'):
- sub-agent PWD: /Users/joeoakhart/digital-service-orchestra/.claude/worktrees/agent-ad25e139
- sub-agent git rev-parse: same isolated path
- canary-ISO.txt created: landed in isolated worktree, NOT in orchestrator session
- Agent result returned worktreePath + worktreeBranch metadata

VERDICT: Platform isolation WORKS RELIABLY in this environment. be6c-ed89's observed file leak cannot be reproduced in controlled conditions today. Hypothesis that 'isolation is inconsistently enforced at the platform layer' is DISPROVED for the current build.

Residual root-cause candidates for the original observation on story 6121-46dc:
(1) The dispatched sub-agent's prompt embedded a cd into the orchestrator root, breaking CWD-based isolation.
(2) The prompt contained absolute paths (e.g., to /Users/joeoakhart/digital-service-orchestra-worktrees/worktree-20260416-132412/...) that bypassed CWD — writes followed the path, not the CWD.
(3) User misattribution — files may have been in the isolated worktree but appeared in the session after a merge/harvest.
(4) A transient platform regression that has since been fixed.

Investigation sub-agent's other confirmed finding: commit 65ffaa44 removed hook_worktree_isolation_guard (the only pre-dispatch enforcement layer). No current hook validates isolation mode. ORCHESTRATOR_ROOT is NOT auto-injected by the platform — it must be added to each dispatch prompt manually. Sprint's task-execution.md has no {orchestrator_root} placeholder.

Recommendation: bug behavior cannot be reproduced; investigation outputs should feed epic c737-977d approach decision rather than drive a standalone fix. Options for be6c-ed89 lifecycle:
(a) Close as unreproducible with this investigation comment as evidence.
(b) Leave open pending defensive fix in epic c737-977d (template enforcement + post-dispatch verification).
(c) Close after a targeted defensive change lands (Fix 1 from investigation: add {orchestrator_root} placeholder to dispatch templates and fail-closed when absent).
